Hardware Integrations#

class bam.dynamixel.actuator.MXActuator(testbench_class: Testbench)[source]#

Represents a Dynamixel MX-64 or MX-106 actuator

initialize()[source]#

Default initialization for kt and R that should be set in a voltage controlled servo

get_extra_inertia() → float[source]#

Get apparent inertia

class bam.dynamixel.actuator.XL320Actuator(testbench_class: Testbench)[source]#

Represents a Dynamixel XL-320 actuator

initialize()[source]#

Default initialization for kt and R that should be set in a voltage controlled servo

get_extra_inertia() → float[source]#

Get apparent inertia

class bam.dynamixel.dynamixel.DynamixelActuatorV1(port: str, id: int = 1)[source]#
set_p_gain(gain: int)[source]#
set_torque(enable: bool)[source]#
set_goal_position(position: float)[source]#
read_data()[source]#
class bam.dynamixel.dynamixel.DynamixelXL320(port: str, id: int = 1)[source]#

Controller for the XL-320 servo motor.

The XL-320 uses Protocol 2.0 only and has a 10-bit position encoder covering a 300° range (0 to 1023 raw, centre = 512 ≡ 0 rad).

Parameters:

  • port – Serial port, e.g. "/dev/ttyUSB0".

  • id – DYNAMIXEL ID (default 1).

set_torque(enable: bool)[source]#

Enable or disable motor torque.

set_p_gain(gain: int)[source]#

Set the proportional (P) gain (0–254).

set_i_gain(gain: int)[source]#

Set the integral (I) gain (0–254).

set_d_gain(gain: int)[source]#

Set the derivative (D) gain (0–254).

set_pid_gains(p: int, i: int, d: int)[source]#

Set P, I and D gains in one call (values 0–254 each).

set_goal_position(position: float)[source]#

Set goal position.

Parameters:

position – Target angle in radians. Acceptable range: [-150°, +150°] → [-2.618, +2.618] rad.

set_moving_speed(speed_rpm: float)[source]#

Set moving speed in Joint Mode.

Parameters:

speed_rpm – Speed in RPM (0 = maximum, 114 = approx. max). The sign is ignored; always positive in joint mode.

set_led(color: int)[source]#

Set the LED colour using a RGB bitmask.

Parameters:

color – Bitmask – Red=1, Green=2, Blue=4 (combinations allowed, 0=off).

read_data() → dict[source]#

Read present position, speed, load, input voltage and temperature.

Returns a dict with keys: position (rad), speed (rad/s), load (signed, 0.1 % units), input_volts (V), temp (°C).

Note: addresses 37–42 are contiguous; 43–44 are unused; 45–46 are voltage and temperature. Two separate reads are performed.

class bam.erob.actuator.ErobActuator(testbench_class: Testbench, damping=2.0)[source]#
initialize()[source]#
load_log(log: dict)[source]#

Called when a log is loaded, can be used to retrieve specific values (eg: input voltage, kp gain, etc.))

control_unit() → str[source]#

Return the unit of the control signal (eg: “volts”, “amps”)

compute_control(q_target: float, q: float, dq: float, dt: float) → float | None[source]#

The control (e.g volts or amps) produced by the actuator, given the position error and current configruation

compute_torque(control: float | None, torque_enable: bool, q: float, dq: float) → float[source]#

The torque [Nm] produced by the actuator, given the control signal and current configuration

get_extra_inertia() → float[source]#

Get apparent inertia

class bam.feetech.actuator.STS3215Actuator(testbench_class: Testbench)[source]#

Feetech STS3215 7.4v

get_extra_inertia() → float[source]#

Get apparent inertia

load_log(log: dict)[source]#

Called when a log is loaded, can be used to retrieve specific values (eg: input voltage, kp gain, etc.))

initialize()[source]#

Default initialization for kt and R that should be set in a voltage controlled servo

compute_control(q_target: float, q: float, dq: float, dt: float) → float | None[source]#

Assumes the motor is using a kp controller This can be overloaded if more custom behaviour is used

load_log(log: dict)[source]#

Called when a log is loaded, can be used to retrieve specific values (eg: input voltage, kp gain, etc.))

control_unit() → str[source]#

Return the unit of the control signal (eg: “volts”, “amps”)

compute_control(q_target: float, q: float, dq: float, dt: float) → float | None[source]#

compute_torque(control: float | None, torque_enable: bool, q: float, dq: float) → float[source]#

The torque [Nm] produced by the actuator, given the control signal and current configuration

get_extra_inertia() → float[source]#
